# ABSOLUTE GOAL

- Give the prompt a code name/"unique identifier": example `20260321-gkid-type` (format: `yyyymmdd-short-description`).
- Generate a complete prompt in #file:../../tmp/yyyymmdd-short-description/prompt.md based on the user input and the context provided by the codebase and database.
- you MUST just generate it, do not execute it yet

## Instructions

- you MUST create a "working folder" in #file:../../tmp/yyyymmdd-short-description/ where `yyyymmdd-short-description` is a "unique identifier" for this prompt.
- you MUST create a file named #file:../../tmp/yyyymmdd-short-description/user-inputs.md in the "working folder" where the user live inputs will be read and tracked during the implementation process.
- you MUST create a directory named #file:../../tmp/yyyymmdd-short-description/tmp/ in the "working folder" where you will store temporary files and intermediate results necessary for the prompt generation and implementation.
- you MUST write the generated prompt in #file:../../tmp/yyyymmdd-short-description/prompt.md with the appropriate YAML front matter and markdown content.
- you MUST use the "critical-loop" on the generated file to ensure that it meets the requirements and is of high quality.
- lastly you must use the SKILL `last-user-input` #file:../skills/last-user-input/SKILL.md to ask the user if they have any last requests or changes before you finalize the prompt and mark the task as complete. This is crucial to ensure that you have met the user's expectations and requirements and that you have not missed any important details. Always confirm with the user before finalizing your prompt to avoid any misalignment or unmet requirements.
